marketState: neutral, conflicting signals — bullish weather/positioning vs bearish storage surplus
summary: Price ($2.885) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.923, FAIR status) across every horizon out to plus21D, so the valuation model offers no directional edge. Seasonality is weak (-15.3% YTD vs -6.9% median, typical4w move -1.4%) but reliability is explicitly flagged Low this week, so it should be weighted lightly, and the calendar is about to roll into September, historically the strongest month. The 8-16 day weather window shows an extreme demand spike (+29.7% vs 5y, above the top of its range) that argues for tighter balances soon, while managed-money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, an unusually crowded short that raises squeeze risk. Working against that: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +22 Bcf surprise, keeping the fundamental backdrop oversupplied. Technicals data is unavailable in this snapshot, which removes a key short-term confirmation tool.

longTerm.rationale: Managed-money net short at the 8th 5y percentile plus an extreme 8-16D heat/demand spike (+29.7% vs 5y, above range max) argue for mean reversion and short-covering as the calendar rolls into seasonally strong September, while price sits below its seasonal median (-8.4pp) offering some value cushion. longTerm.invalidation: A larger-than-expected storage build/surprise, 8-16D heat forecast fading back toward normal, or price breaking decisively below the $2.65-2.60 area would invalidate the long thesis.

shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a technicals feed to confirm trend/momentum, a clear fair-value dislocation (price pushing to a band edge), or the extreme 8-16D heat visibly converting into a spot/near-curve price reaction before entering.
